name: Embalse de Gasset por Las Navas de Magalón — circular desde Magalón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Malagón offers diverse landscapes, from the mountainous Sierra de Malagón to the flat plains surrounding volcanic lagoons. The region features varied terrain, including pine forests, open fields, and extensive valleys with fertile land. Proximity to Cabañeros and Tablas de Daimiel National Parks provides access to Mediterranean forests and unique wetland ecosystems. These natural features create a range of touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Malagón The most popular touring cycling route is River Guadiana Nature Trail Loop, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route follows the course of the River Guadiana through natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de Gasset por Las Navas de Magalón — circular desde Magalón, a moderate 22.9 miles (36.9 km) path. This route explores the Gasset Reservoir and the serene environment of the Las Navas lagoons. Local touring cyclists also love the Río Bañuelos Weir – Río Bañuelos Bridge loop from Malagón, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through the Río Bañuelos area, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Malagón is defined by mountainous terrain, tranquil lagoons, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate routes to more challenging ascents. The routes in Malagón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 37 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Malagón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Malagón?
A: Touring cycling around Malagón offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find mountainous terrain in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Malagón&lt;/strong&gt;, providing climbs and scenic routes, alongside flatter plains surrounding unique volcanic lagoons like those in &lt;strong&gt;Las Navas&lt;/strong&gt;. The region also features extensive valleys with fertile land, pine forests, and open fields, ensuring varied experiences for cyclists.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Malagón?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Malagón, catering to various skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the region's diverse landscapes, from the Sierra de Malagón to the plains and national parks.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Malagón?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Malagón offers several difficult touring cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31523592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrubia de los Ojos – Puerto de los Santos loop from Fuente el Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced riders.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Malagón?
A: Yes, Malagón has routes suitable for beginners. While specific 'easy' routes are not detailed in the top selections, the region's diverse terrain includes flatter sections, particularly around areas like Las Navas and Tablas de Daimiel National Park, which offer more relaxed cycling experiences. Always check the route's elevation profile on komoot for suitability.

Q: What natural features can I explore on touring cycling routes in Malagón?
A: You can explore a wealth of natural features. The routes traverse the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Malagón&lt;/strong&gt; with its pine forests and open fields, and pass by the serene volcanic lagoons of &lt;strong&gt;Las Navas&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is also close to &lt;strong&gt;Cabañeros National Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its Mediterranean forest, and &lt;strong&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/strong&gt;, a unique wetland ecosystem. You might encounter wildlife and enjoy birdwatching, especially around the lagoons.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, touring cycling routes around Malagón offer access to several interesting points. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a significant wetland, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gasset Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation point&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3582372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Picón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Malagón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous climbs to tranquil lagoons, and the peaceful rural environment with low traffic. The opportunity to connect with nature and explore significant natural parks is also a highlight.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Malagón?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Malagón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352213759/embalse-de-gasset-por-las-navas-de-magalon-circular-desde-magalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Gasset por Las Navas de Magalón — circular desde Magalón&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Gasset Reservoir and the Las Navas lagoons.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Malagón?
A: Malagón and its surrounding areas, being a rural region, generally offer accessible parking options in towns and near trailheads. Specific parking facilities may vary by route, but the low population density often makes finding a suitable spot easier for cyclists starting their tours.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Malagón?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn are ideal for touring cycling in Malagón. During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder and more comfortable for longer rides, allowing you to fully enjoy the diverse landscapes without the extreme heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or water bodies?
A: Yes, you can find touring cycling routes that follow rivers and explore water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058436687/river-guadiana-nature-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River Guadiana Nature Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, following the course of the River Guadiana through natural landscapes. Additionally, routes around Las Navas explore the serene volcanic lagoons.

Q: Can I combine cultural visits with touring cycling in Malagón?
A: Absolutely. Beyond its natural beauty, Malagón has historical significance, including connections to Santa Teresa de Jesús and a local museum. Many routes are designed to connect small towns, allowing you to integrate cultural exploration and visits to local points of interest into your cycling adventures.

The name of this town is attributed to the reconquerer and first lord of it, whose version was written at the beginning of the 14th century as Ferrant Cavallero.
The town of Fernán Caballero develops around the Plaza Mayor, triangular in shape, where the Church of Nuestra Señora de Gracia from the 16th century is located, with a double-braced wooden roof, a classicist portal to the south and a square tower to the south. west, culminating in a modernist bell tower and clock
